title: Individual Agencies - Accurint/LexisNexis Data Contributions (Alaska Department of Public Safety)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Alaska Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1) All contracts, addendums (e.g. AVCC XML Addendum), MOUs, and schedules (e.g. Schedule A) and legal documents signed between your agency and LexisNexis (and owner RELX or subsidiaries, including LexisNexis Risk Solutions, Accurint, AVCC, PSDEX, etc.), or between your agency and a law enforcement consortium that provides you access to AVCC (e.g. AVCC Sub-Agency Agreement). 
2) Agency Configuration Page: A printout of your agency's AVCC configuration page. Please include the full page, including any other agencies listed on the same page. Example Screenshot: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1G_J35fnmu7qZO_k8UOoVHFcd_R2HKXVg/view?usp=sharing

We understand that you are not required to respond to questions, however if you are willing, could you please answer the following:
A) Does your agency contribute / share data with Accurint or the LexisNexis Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX) [If no, skip remaining questions]
B) Do you restrict who your data is shared with - and what are those agencies? 
C) Do you share data belonging to both adults and minors, or do you restrict sharing by age? 
D) Are you able to audit how your agency's contributed data is used by others?

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: CVISS Minutes and LexisNexis/Accurint Data Sharing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. The current Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) governing the Central Valley Information Sharing System (CVISS), including all participating agency joinder agreements and the full list of current member agencies.

2. CVISS Meeting minutes for 2025 and 2026 CVISS meetings. 

3. All current contracts, addendums, amendments, and schedules signed with LexisNexis (aka RELX / LexisNexis Risk Management / Accurint). This includes The 'Accurint Virtual Crime Center / Accurint Crime Analysis / LexisNexis Community Crime Map / AVCC XML Addendum' or 'PSDEX Addendum' executed by the Sacramento County Sheriff's Office as Lead Agency for the CVISS consortium, including all exhibits and schedules (e.g. Schedule A).

4. Any 'AVCC XML / PSDEX Addendum' or 'Member Agency Addendum and Joinder' executed by any CVISS member agency in your possession.

5) Agency Page: A printout of your agency's AVCC configuration page. Please include the full page, including any other agencies listed on the same page. Example Screenshot: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1G_J35fnmu7qZO_k8UOoVHFcd_R2HKXVg/view?usp=sharing

Context: We understand that CVISS moved to Accurint to share data among CVISS members. We are seeking to understand whether Sacramento County Sheriff and other CVISS members share data with agencies beyond the consortium (PSDEX / National Database) or restricts data sharing to CVISS members only.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: LexisNexis Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC) PSDEX Data Sharing Agreement (Austin Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This request is focused on Accurint Virtual Crime Center (AVCC) and Public Safety Data Exchange (PSDEX), both are products of LexisNexis Risk Solutions (LNRS / RELX). We are making this request because we found evidence that your agency likely uses, has access to, or contributes data to AVCC (directly or indirectly). If your agency has no relationship with these products, please tell us so.

We are seeking records that indicate whether your agency contributes data to AVCC/PSDEX, and if so, what other agencies have access to that data. 
--
We are requesting the following records:
1) All signed contracts, addendums, amendments, or agreements between your agency and LexisNexis that are currently in effect or were in effect at any point between 1/1/2020 and the date this request is fulfilled.  
[CONTEXT] Common documents include "AVCC XML Addendum" / "Schedule A" / "Master Terms & Conditions" / "sub-agency agreements" / "joinder agreements" or other similar agreements. If your agency contributes data or gets AVCC access through a consortium or fusion center, please include the MOU or similar agreement outlining that AVCC relationship. 
2) AVCC Usage Logs. 
 - 2.A) If your agency shares data with AVCC/PSDEX, please provide query logs of who has accessed your agency's data and what reason/case number they provided while searching your agency's records. We understand some of these reports are generated by LexisNexis rather than stored locally; please include any such report your agency has received or can obtain. Please provide in CSV or XLSX format where available. If you are unable to see logs of how your agency's data is used by other agencies within AVCC, please indicate so. 
3) AVCC Configuration:
 - 3.A) Agency Configuration Page: A printout of your agency's AVCC configuration page. Please include the full page, including any other agencies listed on the same page. Example Screenshot: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1G_J35fnmu7qZO_k8UOoVHFcd_R2HKXVg/view?usp=sharing 
 - 3.B) Configuration Explainer Documents: Typically documents with details on what data LexisNexis recommends that you contribute, and what they recommend you exclude. Sometimes this may be called "FAQs and FYIs for Exclusions", or may include integration suggestions for an interface with your RMS/JMS/CAD systems. 
--
PRIORITY: The two most important documents we seek are your signed AVCC XML Addendum and 3.A. - The AVCC agency configuration page printout. 
SEARCH TERMS: To aid in your search, we recommend the following search terms: "AVCC" "Accurint" "PSDEX" "Public Safety Data Exchange" "Public Safety Marketplace" and "Accurint Virtual Crime Center"

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Shawn Segal

title: EEOC and Opexus

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: EEOC and Opexus
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

This is narrowed request relative to my first request about the same matter. Narrowed Request: For dates January 1, 2022 to February 28, 2026, I request all correspondence between EEOC and Opexus management and staff - including staff and management from AINS, which is the former company name of Opexus.

Background:
On March 3, 2026, I responded to EEOC's February 26, 2026 acknowledgment notice (or as stated by EEOC to be on February 27, 2026) and stated: "Regarding # 820-2026-005851, I thank you for the response. I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA Office. As a standing request, ask that EEOC not require me to log into a potentially compromised FOIA system to view any updates , etc. This is especially salient because of recent serious security concerns.... Thank you for considering my requests and desire for secure and transparent communications." Again I do not have an account with EEOC's FOIA portal and I elect not to use it for reasons I have explained. I request that all communications occur outside of the EEOC FOIA platform and be transparently sent for public view.

On April 9, 2026, EEOC Office of Legal Counsel sent a letter stating among other things, "This letter is to inform you that we would like to have a conversation with you in hopes of clarifying and narrowing your request. We conducted a search on February 27, 2026, based on your request as written, which resulted in over 124,000 items. We do not have an exact page count, but assuming that doing an industry standard of about three (3)pages per item, the actual page count is going to be likely around the neighborhood of over 372,000 pages. We are putting this request on hold so that we may have a conversation with you concerning how you can effectively limit the scope of this request, so that it can be processed. " Unfortunately EEOC only provided me with 10 days to respond. I was on annual leave during these 10 days. Thus, I am resubmitting this modified request.

On May 10, 2026, I resubmitted a narrow request resulting in a May 28, 2026 rejection. Please consider my reasonable accommodation request of at least 20 days to respond to any notices through the MuckRock portal. Also, I ask that in the future, that you not close any of my requests if I do not respond in less than 20 days. This is a standing request. title: Flock Safety Organization and Network Logs | Q2 2026 | American Fork Police Department

communications.subject: Government Records Access and Management Act Request: Flock Safety Organization and Network Logs | Q2 2026 | American Fork Police Department
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.

The report should include data logged from the period of 1/1/2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.

The report should include data logged from the period of 1/1/2025, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

Please provide the audit data in a structured format such as .csv, .xls, or another machine-readable database filetype.

3. Data Sharing.

3a. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares ALPR data;

3b. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives ALPR data;

3c. The names of agencies and organizations with which the Agency shares “hot list” information;

3d. The names of agencies and organizations from which the Agency receives “hot list” information.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

a
